cards.title: Emergency Caesarean Births Rise to One in Four
cards.body: BBC analysis reveals a significant increase in emergency caesarean sections in England over the past five years, with one in four births now delivered this way. Experts say there's no single clear explanation for the rise, but the shift marks an important trend in childbirth outcomes.

cards.title: How to Self-Soothe: Coregulation and Baby Development
cards.body: A baby's ability to self-soothe depends on age, distress level, and temperament, meaning parental help isn't a bad habit—it's essential development. Understanding coregulation shows why soothing your child is actually teaching them crucial emotional skills.

cards.title: Parents Often Sidelined in Child Mental Health Treatment
cards.body: Research shows parents are frequently left out of their child's mental health care decisions and support. Strengthening parental involvement may be an overlooked pathway to better outcomes for struggling children and teens.

cards.title: Supporting Grieving Children Through Parental Loss
cards.body: Grieving children need all the comfort available to preserve a parent's legacy as they navigate life without them. Creating meaningful ways to remember helps children process loss and maintain connection.

cards.title: Family Scapegoats Often Tell Uncomfortable Truths
cards.body: If you were the family scapegoat, you may have been blamed for seeing dysfunction others denied. This painful role actually reflects important strengths in recognizing and speaking truth.

cards.title: Teaching Boys and Girls Differently Gets Best Results
cards.body: Former England manager Gareth Southgate created a documentary examining how boys and young men develop differently. His work suggests tailored approaches to education and mentoring could better support each gender's needs.

cards.title: Understanding Child Diagnosis: Identity and Emotions
cards.body: A diagnosis can shape a child's identity, expectations, and emotions, and acceptance doesn't happen instantly. Supporting families along the way requires understanding that this is a complex emotional journey.

cards.title: Family Estrangement Effects Are More Nuanced Than Expected
cards.body: Research shows estrangement exists on a continuum rather than being a binary cut-off, with mixed effects on wellbeing. Support plays a crucial role whether or not people seek to reconcile with estranged family.

cards.title: Affordable Childcare Is Essential for Raising Children
cards.body: Government spending on childcare support is meant to help families, but some profiteers are preventing people from getting full benefits. Policymakers warn against allowing cost barriers to prevent families from having children.

cards.title: Adult Children Navigate Caregiving for Aging Parents
cards.body: Caring for an aging parent is an expected but often stressful phase of adulthood with competing needs. Balancing a parent's autonomy, their safety, and your own wellbeing requires thoughtful planning and boundaries.
